elements.text: B5-5.2-01, Loans With Resale Restrictions: General Information (05/03/2023)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains general information on loans with resale restrictions, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Allowable Resale Restrictions
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Duration of Resale Restrictions
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Title Insurance Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Default Remedies
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Rights to Insurance Settlements and Condemnation Proceeds
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Resale restrictions are a right in perpetuity or for a certain number of years, stated in the form of a restriction, easement, covenant, or condition in any deed, mortgage, ground lease, agreement, or other instrument executed by or on behalf of the owner of the land. The resale restrictions may limit the occupancy of all or part of the land to individuals meeting certain conditions, such as age (senior communities must comply with applicable law). When resale restrictions are included in a mortgage, the terms must comply with this Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: This topic does not pertain to loans that are part of shared equity transactions, which have resale restrictions that limit both income eligibility and impose a maximum resale price. See Section B5-5.3, Shared Equity Transactions for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Allowable Resale Restrictions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae will purchase loans that are subject to one or more of the following types of resale restrictions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: income limits,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: age-related requirements,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: employment-related requirements,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: occupancy requirements,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: first-time homebuyer requirements, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: restrictions on group homes or homes that are principally used to serve residents with disabilities.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Some restrictions are likely to occur only in combination with others and it is permissible for more than one resale restriction to apply to a single loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Duration of Resale Restrictions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae will purchase loans secured by properties subject to resale restrictions in the following circumstances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: when the restrictions terminate automatically upon foreclosure (or the expiration of any applicable redemption period),
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: upon the recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: when the resale restrictions survive foreclosure.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Note: If the resale restrictions survive foreclosure, the lender represents and warrants that the resale restrictions do not impair the servicer's ability to foreclose on the restricted property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: There are no restrictions on the length of the period in which the resale restrictions may remain in place on the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Title Insurance Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The source and terms of the resale restrictions must be included in the public land records so that they are readily identifiable in a routine title search.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default Remedies
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The presence of resale restrictions must not impair Fannie Mae's legal rights to cure a default under the loan terms, to foreclose on the subject property, or to otherwise protect Fannie Mae's interests under the mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rights to Insurance Settlements and Condemnation Proceeds
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae must have first claim to insurance settlements and condemnation proceeds.
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on loans with resale restrictions loan and borrower eligibility, collateral and delivery requirements including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligibility Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Appraisal Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Pricing and Delivery Considerations
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Eligibility Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following table describes the eligibility requirements for loans with resale restrictions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Criteria
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The loan must be a fixed-rate or ARM with an initial fixed period of five years or more
elements.label: text

elements.text: and must comply with this Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers must meet applicable criteria of the deed restriction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If income limit requirements are imposed by both the resale restrictions and the terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the mortgage, the most restrictive will apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Age-related restrictions generally apply to the unit occupant and frequently
elements.label: text

elements.text: require only one occupant to be aged 55 and over. In such a case, the borrower could
elements.label: text

elements.text: be younger than 55 provided there is a unit occupant aged 55 and over. This
elements.label: text

elements.text: occupant can be a non-borrower household member or a renter in the case of an
elements.label: text

elements.text: investment property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Property Type
elements.label: text

elements.text: One- and two-unit properties are eligible, including PUDs, condos, and co-ops.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured homes are not eligible unless they are located in a PERS-approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisal Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: In cases where the resale restrictions terminate automatically upon foreclosure (or the expiration of any applicable redemption period), or upon recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the appraisal should reflect the market value of the property without resale restrictions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The lender must ensure that the borrower and appraiser are aware of the resale restrictions and should advise the appraiser that they must include the following statement in the appraisal report:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: "This appraisal is made on the basis of a hypothetical condition that the property rights being appraised are without resale and other restrictions that are terminated automatically upon the latter of foreclosure or the expiration of any applicable redemption period, or upon recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure."
elements.label: text

elements.text: In cases where the resale restrictions survive foreclosure or a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the appraisal must reflect the impact the restrictions have on value and be supported by comparable sales with similar restrictions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must note the existence of the resale restrictions and comment on any impact the resale restrictions have on the property's value and marketability.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pricing and Delivery Considerations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Pricing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: There are no specific LLPAs required for loans with resale restrictions. All other price adjustments that are otherwise applicable to the transaction will apply. For a list of current applicable LLPAs, see the Loan-Level Price Adjustment (LLPA) Matrix .
elements.label: text

elements.text: Special Feature Codes
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders must report SFC 631 when delivering a loan secured by a property with resale restrictions that survive foreclosure or a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: No special feature code is required when delivering a loan secured by a property with resale restrictions that terminate automatically upon foreclosure (or the expiration of any applicable redemption period), or the recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure unless the loan is part of a shared equity transaction. See B5-5.3-03, Shared Equity Transactions: Eligibility, Underwriting and Collateral Requirements  for additional information.

elements.text: B5-5.3-01, Shared Equity Overview (02/05/2025)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on shared equity transactions, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Community Land Trusts
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Income and Resale Price Restrictions
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Shared equity programs, sometimes called "below-market programs," create long-term affordability by both limiting income eligibility and imposing a maximum resale price (or a resale formula applies that limits the homeowner's proceeds upon resale). Shared equity providers typically make a one-time investment in a property by subsidizing the sales price for the first buyer and recycling the subsidy for subsequent, incomeeligible buyers of the same property. In exchange for purchasing a home below market value, the homeowners agree to restrictions on the future sale of the home, including limitations on sales price or proceeds that maintain affordability for the next buyer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans originated in connection with the following shared equity programs are eligible for purchase by Fannie Mae:
elements.label: text

elements.text: community land trusts,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: income and resale price restrictions,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: limited or shared equity co-ops.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: It is against Fannie Mae policy to compel borrowers to participate in arbitration to resolve disputes regarding their mortgage loan. See  B8-3-02, Special Note Provisions and Language Requirements. Similarly, Fannie Mae will not purchase shared equity loans where the borrower is required to submit to arbitration to resolve disputes with the shared equity program unless the mandatory arbitration provision provides, or is amended to provide, that, in the event of a transfer or sale of the related mortgage or an interest in the related mortgage loan to Fannie Mae, the mandatory arbitration clause immediately and automatically becomes null and void and cannot be reinstated. This does not prohibit the borrower and the shared equity program from engaging voluntarily in arbitration, mediation, or other alternative methods of dispute resolution.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: This topic focuses on community land trusts and income and resale price restrictions. For information on limited or shared equity co-ops, see B4-2.1-03, Ineligible Projects.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trusts
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Community land trusts preserve long-term affordable housing by purchasing homes in their communities, then selling the homes to income-eligible buyers while retaining ownership of the land. The homeowners lease the land from the community land trust using a long-term ground lease with affordable monthly ground rents. The ground lease includes provisions that preserve long-term affordability, including restrictions on the future sales price of the home and income levels of future homeowners.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Income and Resale Price Restrictions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Resale restrictions are a right in perpetuity or for a certain number of years and are binding on current and future property owners. They remain in effect until they are formally removed or modified, or terminate in accordance with their terms, such as at a foreclosure sale or upon acceptance of a deed-in-lieu.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Income and resale price restrictions help maintain long-term affordability for shared equity programs. These programs include both an income and a maximum resale price restriction that effectively subsidize the sales prices for income-eligible buyers, and in exchange for the subsidy, the buyers agree to resale restrictions that limit the future sales price of the home to preserve affordability for future income-eligible buyers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For information on other types of resale restrictions, see  B5-5.2-01, Loans With Resale Restrictions: General Information  and B5-5.2-02, Loans with Resale Restrictions: Eligibility, Collateral and Delivery Requirements.

elements.text: Notification to Third Parties
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The terms of the shared equity legal agreement (as modified by a Community Land Trust Ground Rider , if applicable) may require the servicer to notify an interested third party, such as the shared equity provider or its designee, when the borrower defaults on the loan or in the event of foreclosure. In this case, the servicer must ensure proper notification is provided, as required by the terms of the legal agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of any third-party notification requirements, the servicer is still responsible for adhering to Fannie Mae's established timeframes for completing routine foreclosure. Any third-party notifications required in addition to the required statutory notifications must not impair the servicer's ability to foreclose.

elements.text: Affordable LTV Calculation: Resale Restrictions that Terminate at Foreclosure
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When resale restrictions terminate automatically upon foreclosure (or the expiration of any applicable redemption period), or the recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the sales price is typically not a reliable indicator of market value for the property because the sales price does not include the subsidy from the shared equity provider. The appraised value is more indicative of the actual value of the property in the event of a foreclosure or acceptance of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure (disregarding factors that may affect value after origination and prior to foreclosure). Accordingly, for these types of transactions, lenders must use the "Affordable LTV" calculation to determine LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV ratios.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Affordable LTV calculation divides the loan amount by the appraised value of the property, rather than the lesser of the sales price or the appraised value. The following table provides an example of the Affordable LTV calculation method.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Item
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraised value
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shared equity program subsidy amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subsidized sales price
elements.label: text

elements.text: First mortgage loan amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: Affordable LTV Ratio (first mortgage loan amount divided by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraised value)
elements.label: text

elements.text: When using the Affordable LTV calculation, the lender must
elements.label: text

elements.text: for DU loans, enter "Affordable LTV" in the Product Description field in the online application, which will result in DU calculating the LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV ratios based solely on the appraised value for purchase transactions (and not the lesser of the sales price or appraised value); and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: for all loans, use the appraised value to determine
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the minimum down payment;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the borrower contribution, if applicable, that must be made from the borrower's own resources; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the level of mortgage insurance required.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: LTV Calculation: Resale Restrictions that Survive Foreclosure
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When resale restrictions survive foreclosure or a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the lender must use the lesser of the sales price or appraised value of the property with resale restrictions when calculating the LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV ratios, which is the standard method of calculation. Fannie Mae requires use of the standard calculation on the lower value due to the presence of resale restrictions, which limit the property's sales price in the event of foreclosure or acceptance of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Note: This does not apply to loans secured by community land trust properties, which require the resale restrictions to terminate automatically upon foreclosure (or the expiration of any applicable redemption period), or acceptance of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisal Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For properties in a community land trust, the appraisal requirements can be found in  B4-1.4-06, Community Land Trust Appraisal Requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For properties with income and resale price restrictions, the following requirements apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: In cases where the resale restrictions terminate automatically upon foreclosure (or the expiration of any applicable redemption period), or upon recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the appraisal should reflect the market value of the property without resale restrictions. The lender must ensure that the borrower and appraiser are aware of the resale restrictions and should advise the appraiser that they must include the following statement in the appraisal report:
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: "This appraisal is made on the basis of a hypothetical condition that the property rights being appraised are without resale and other restrictions that are terminated automatically upon the latter of foreclosure or the expiration of any applicable redemption period, or upon recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure."
elements.label: text

elements.text: In cases where the resale restrictions survive foreclosure or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the appraisal must reflect the impact the restrictions have on value and be supported by comparable sales with similar restrictions. The appraisal report must note the existence of the resale restrictions and comment on any impact the resale restrictions have on the property's value and marketability.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: B5-5.3-04, Massachusetts Resale Restriction Loan Eligibility Requirements (05/03/2023)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on Massachusetts resale restriction loan eligibility requirements, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender Eligibility
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Loan Eligibility Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Legal Considerations
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lender Eligibility
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae may purchase loans in Massachusetts that are subject to the Affordable Housing Restriction document without further approval from Fannie Mae. This includes any federal, state, or local subsidy program for properties located in Massachusetts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Use of the Affordable Housing Restriction document (copies of which can be obtained from the Massachusetts Housing Finance Agency) without modification, alteration or update, other than filling in the blanks or checking boxes contained in the form, eliminates the need for the lender to review the terms and conditions of the particular subsidy program with respect to loans delivered to Fannie Mae secured by properties in Massachusetts that are subject to resale restriction(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Eligibility Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The Massachusetts Housing Finance Agency requires the use of an Affordable Housing Restriction document in connection with loans secured by properties in Massachusetts that are subject to resale restrictions that survive foreclosure or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure in which it will serve as lender or project administrator.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Legal Considerations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The Affordable Housing Restriction document provides for third-party modification by the foreclosing servicer and provides for a 120-day time period between notification of foreclosure to the municipality by the foreclosing servicer and the completion of the repurchase of the property by the municipality.
elements.label: text

elements.text: While Fannie Mae's standard guidelines require a 90-day time period for notification, use of this instrument for loans subject to a resale restriction secured by property in Massachusetts is acceptable without further approval from Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All other applicable requirements for resale restrictions continue to apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The HomeReady mortgage is a conventional community lending mortgage that offers underwriting flexibilities to qualified borrowers who meet specific income criteria. The HomeReady mortgage is a standard product offering available to all Fannie Mae lenders. No special approvals are required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: General Loan Eligibility
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A HomeReady mortgage is a first mortgage, purchase money, or limited cash-out refinance transaction for oneto four-unit properties used as the borrower's principal residence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible properties include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: one-unit properties, including manufactured housing, and units in condos and PUDs;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: units in co-ops, provided the unit conforms to Fannie Mae's requirements, and the lender has received specific authority to deliver mortgages on co-ops to Fannie Mae;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: existing structures and new construction; and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: two-, three-, and four-unit properties.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Additional restrictions apply to transactions with LTV, CLTV, or HCLTV ratios of 95.01 - 97%. See below for additional requirements for HomeReady mortgage transactions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Maximum LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV Ratios
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to the Eligibility Matrix for maximum allowable LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV ratios for HomeReady mortgage loans. HomeReady loans that are originated in connection with manufactured homes must follow the more restrictive LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV ratios that apply. For example, the maximum LTV, CLTV, and HCLTV ratio for a one-unit HomeReady manufactured home that does not meet the MH Advantage requirements is 95%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements for HomeReady Transactions with LTV, CLTV, or HCLTV Ratios of 95.01 - 97%
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: If the LTV, CLTV, or HCLTV ratio exceeds 95% for a HomeReady transaction, the following requirements apply.

elements.text: Borrower Income Limits and Calculations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: In determining whether a mortgage is eligible under the borrower income limits, the lender must count the income from all borrowers who will sign the note, to the extent that the income is considered in evaluating creditworthiness for the loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The lender must use the same methodology in determining income eligibility for a HomeReady mortgage as the lender uses in reporting 'Monthly Income' in data delivery. Eligibility for a HomeReady mortgage loan compares the borrower's income to the applicable area median income (AMI) for the property's location. For determining Fannie Mae loan eligibility, lenders must refer to the AMIs that Fannie Mae uses in Desktop Underwriter or on Fannie Mae's website and may not rely on other published versions (such as AMIs posted on huduser.org).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: To be eligible as a HomeReady mortgage, the total annual qualifying income may not exceed 80% of the AMI for the property's location. If the property has resale restrictions, see B5-5.2-02, Loans with Resale Restrictions: Eligibility, Collateral and Delivery Requirements, for additional requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note : For loan casefiles that are not underwritten as a HomeReady mortgage loan, DU will issue a message indicating that the loan may be eligible as a HomeReady loan if the total qualifying income entered in DU appears to be within the applicable AMI limit for the property's location. See  B5-6-02, HomeReady Mortgage Underwriting Methods and Requirements, for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Homeownership Education and Housing Counseling
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Homeownership education is required for HomeReady purchase loans when all occupying borrowers are firsttime homebuyers. Borrowers who complete housing counseling within 12 months prior to loan closing may be eligible for a loan-level price adjustment credit. Refer to B2-2-06, Homeownership Education and Housing Counseling, for options for meeting these requirements.

elements.text: Underwriting Options
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: HomeReady mortgage loans can be underwritten with DU or may be manually underwritten. The maximum LTV ratio is lower for manually underwritten transactions versus those underwritten in DU (95% versus 97% for oneunit principal residences). As a reminder, the limited waiver of representations and warranties typically granted for loans underwritten with DU does not apply to manually underwritten loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For HomeReady mortgage loans that are underwritten through DU, the lender must enter data in the online loan application, identify the loan as a community lending mortgage, and select the HomeReady product.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the lender does not select HomeReady as the community lending product, DU will provide a message when the total qualifying income entered in DU appears to be within the applicable AMI limits for the property's location. The lender must then select the HomeReady product and resubmit the loan casefile to help determine if the loan meets all of the HomeReady requirements (assuming the lender wants to sell the loan to Fannie Mae as a HomeReady mortgage).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026

elements.text: Non-Occupant Borrowers
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Non-occupant borrowers are permitted on HomeReady mortgages. See B2-2-04, Guarantors, Co-Signers, or NonOccupant Borrowers on the Subject Transaction, for the eligibility requirements that apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental Income from the Subject Property
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Rental income is an acceptable source of qualifying income in the following instances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: one-unit principal residence with an accessory unit. See B4-1.3-05, Improvements Section of the Appraisal Report, for additional details related to acceptable accessory units;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: two- to four-unit principal residence properties.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: See  B3-3.8-01, Rental Income, for calculation and documentation of rental income used for qualifying purposes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Boarder Income
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The rental payments that any borrower receives from one or more individuals who reside with the borrower (who may or may not be related to the borrower) may be considered as acceptable stable income. This applies for a one-unit property in an amount up to 30% of the total gross income that is used to qualify the borrower for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: the mortgage if the boarder
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: is not obligated on the mortgage loan and does not have an ownership interest in the property;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: has lived with the borrower for the last 12 months;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: can provide appropriate documentation to demonstrate a history of shared residency (such as a copy of a driver's license, bill, or bank statement that shows the boarder's address as being the same as the borrower's address); and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: can demonstrate the payment of rental payments (such as with copies of canceled checks) to the borrower for
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the last 12 months, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: at least 9 of the most recent 12 months provided the rental income is averaged over a 12-month period.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Payment of rent by the boarder directly to a third party is not acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash-on-Hand
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Lenders may deliver purchase money mortgages for one-unit properties with cash-on-hand as an acceptable source of funds for the borrower's down payment, funds for closing costs, and prepaid items.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note : Cash-on-hand may not be used to fund the borrower's reserve requirement, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The lender must verify and document the following with respect to the cash-on-hand funds:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The borrower customarily uses cash for expenses, and the amount of funds saved is consistent with the borrower's previous payment practices.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender must verify that funds for the down payment and closing costs exist in a financial institution account or an acceptable escrow account. Funds must be on deposit at the time of application, or no less than 30 days prior to closing.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender must obtain a written statement from the borrower that discloses the source of funds and states that the funds have not been borrowed.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The borrower's credit report and other verifications should indicate limited or no use of credit and limited or no depository relationship between the borrower and a financial institution.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Sweat Equity
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae considers sweat equity an acceptable source of funds for HomeReady loans when the borrower participates in an affordable housing purchase program run by an eligible provider. Sweat equity program providers must be a nonprofit organization exempt from taxation under Section 501(c)(3) of the IRS code with a demonstrated history of affordable housing construction and experience in managing volunteers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sweat equity can only be applied towards the down payment, and the borrower must comply with the requirements in Minimum Borrower Contribution for Purchase Transactions .
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum Reserve Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For manually underwritten loans, the reserve requirements are documented in the Eligibility Matrix . For DU loan casefiles, DU will determine the reserve requirement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Multiple Financed Properties
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The occupant borrower may not have more than two financed properties. See B2-2-03, Multiple Financed Properties for the Same Borrower, for the requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers with Low Credit Scores: Manual Underwriting Only
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For manually underwritten HomeReady mortgage loans secured by one-unit properties, if the loan-level credit score is less than the minimum credit score required for a HomeReady mortgage, the loan may still be eligible for purchase by Fannie Mae if the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The credit report indicates that the borrower's credit score is low due to an insufficient traditional credit history (as documented by reason codes on the credit report that indicate a lack of credit accounts, accounts not opened long enough, lack of usage, etc., as reasons for the low credit score). If the borrower's credit score is low due to derogatory credit or if none of the reason codes noted above appear on the credit report, then the minimum credit score for the transaction must be met (per the Eligibility Matrix).
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender must supplement the traditional credit file (referred to as a 'thin file') with the development of an acceptable nontraditional credit profile in accordance with Section B3-5.4, Nontraditional Credit History.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The lender must deliver the borrower's credit score (even if below the minimum required) along with SFC 818 at loan delivery to identify HomeReady mortgage loans that have borrowers with thin files.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Note: Special Feature Code 818 should only be used to indicate a 'thin file' HomeReady mortgage loan.

elements.text: Chapter B5-7, High Loan-to-Value Refinance Option
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter provides the policies and requirements for the high loan-to-value refinance option.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B5-7-01, High LTV Refinance Loan and Borrower Eligibility (08/06/2025)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information about the following aspects of the high LTV refinance option, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Existing Loan Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: New Loan Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Borrower Eligibility
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Property Eligibility
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: LTV Ratio Requirements
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Eligible Subordinate Financing
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Leasehold Estates Eligibility
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Multiple Financed Properties
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The high LTV refinance option is designed for Fannie Mae borrowers who are making their mortgage payments on time, but whose LTV ratios exceed the maximum allowed for standard limited cash-out refinance transactions. Lenders are not required to evaluate borrower creditworthiness except for the requirements specifically stated in the high LTV refinance topics.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The current servicer or a new servicer may refinance the existing loan. Lenders may not solicit Fannie Mae loans for refinancing except in accordance with standard requirements in Lender Solicitation for Refinancing found in B2-1.3-04, Prohibited Refinancing Practices.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The acquisition of high LTV refinances is currently paused.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Existing Loan Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following table provides requirements for the existing loan that is to be refinanced under the high LTV refinance option.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The existing loan must....
elements.label: text

elements.text: be a first lien, conventional mortgage loan owned or securitized by Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: have a note date on or after October 1, 2017.
elements.label: text

elements.text: have seasoning of at least 15 months - meaning at least 15 months have passed from the note
elements.label: text

elements.text: date of the existing loan to the note date of the new loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For example, if the note date on the existing loan is January 1, 2018, the note date of the new
elements.label: text

elements.text: loan must be no earlier than April 1, 2019.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Loans that are part of a risk-sharing structure (for example, credit risk transfers) are eligible to be refinanced under the high LTV refinance option.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Conversely, the following loans are not eligible for refinance under the high LTV refinance option:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: existing DU Refi Plus TM or Refi Plus TM loans;
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the new loan is also subject to a credit enhancement that meets eligibility requirements, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: such credit enhancement is not required for eligibility purposes on the new loan.

elements.text: Borrower Eligibility
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Generally, the borrower(s) on the loan being refinanced (or the current borrower(s) if the existing loan was assumed) must be identical to the borrower(s) on the new loan. However, an existing borrower may be excluded from the new loan for either of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the remaining borrower(s) meets the mortgage payment history requirements and provides evidence that they have been making the payments on the existing loan from their own funds for the most recent 12 months prior to the application of the new loan, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: due to the death of a borrower. Evidence of the deceased borrower's death must be documented in the loan file.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: If this criteria cannot be met, the new loan must be underwritten in accordance with the Alternative Qualification Path. See B5-7-03, High LTV Refinance Alternative Qualification Path for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: New borrowers may not be added to the new loan refinanced via the high LTV refinance option. Additionally, if the loan being refinanced was assumed by the current borrower(s) prior to the refinance, the current borrowers must have been qualified for the existing loan in accordance with the requirements of the Servicing Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers who have applied for or received a modification are eligible for refinancing provided the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the borrower benefit provision is met using the prevailing payment, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the payment history requirement is met. See Credit Eligibility Requirements in B5-7-02, High LTV Refinance Underwriting, Documentation, and Collateral Requirements for the New Loan .
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Property Eligibility
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: All Fannie Mae-eligible property types are permitted for refinance under the high LTV refinance option.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For properties in condo, co-op, or PUD projects, all project review requirements are waived with the exception that the lender must confirm the project is not a condo or co-op hotel or motel, houseboat project, timeshare, or segmented ownership project. For assistance in determining whether the project is a condo or co-op hotel or motel, see  B4-2.1-03, Ineligible Projects.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The lender must obtain property and flood insurance in accordance with this Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Subordinate Financing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: New subordinate financing is only permitted if it replaces existing subordinate financing. In addition, the existing subordinate financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: may not be satisfied with the proceeds of the new loan, but
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: may remain in place as long as it is resubordinated to the new loan, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: may be simultaneously refinanced as long as the new subordinate lien loan amount does not exceed the existing UPB.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Other subordinate financing requirements described in B2-1.2-04, Subordinate Financing are not applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Leasehold Estates Eligibility
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The term of the leasehold must run for at least five years beyond the maturity date of the mortgage, unless fee simple title will vest at an earlier date in the borrower. The lender is not required to perform any additional review of the leasehold terms.

elements.text: Multiple Financed Properties
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: There are no limits on the number of financed properties the borrower may own. The additional eligibility requirements for borrowers with multiple financed properties in B2-2-03, Multiple Financed Properties for the Same Borrower do not apply.

elements.text: Underwriting Methods
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: High LTV refinance loans may be underwritten using DU or manually, with the exception of loans subject to the Alternative Qualification Path (which must be manually underwritten). Loans secured by a second home or an investment property must be underwritten in DU and receive an Approve/Eligible recommendation, unless they are required to be underwritten in accordance with the Alternative Qualification Path (see B5-7-03, High LTV Refinance Alternative Qualification Path).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans Underwritten in DU
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: When a limited cash-out refinance loan casefile that meets the minimum LTV requirement for a high LTV refinance loan is underwritten in DU, internal data will be used to determine if Fannie Mae owns the loan on the property, and if that loan is eligible to be refinanced using the high LTV refinance option.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: When DU finds a loan for the subject property address using either the address provided on the DU loan application or the standardized address, DU will then confirm that the Social Security number(s) for the borrower(s) on the new loan casefile match those on the existing loan. The result of the Social Security number matching will be specified in a DU message.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When none of the borrower Social Security numbers match, the loan casefile will not be underwritten as a high LTV refinance loan. DU will issue a message informing the lender that the Social Security number(s) does not match and remind the lender to confirm the property address.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When there is a Social Security number match, DU will underwrite the loan casefile as a high LTV refinance loan. If the Social Security number for any of the borrowers on the loan casefile do not match using all nine digits, DU will advise the lender.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a borrower Social Security number is matched using 7 or 8 digits of the 9-digit Social Security number, the DU message will specify that the Social Security numbers are one or two digits different and will require the lender to confirm the borrowers on the existing loan are the same borrowers that will be on the new loan.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: When there are two borrowers on the new loan and two borrowers on the existing loan, but only one borrower's Social Security number matches, the DU message will specify that not all of the borrower Social Security numbers match and will require that the lender confirm the borrowers on the existing loan are the same borrowers that will be on the new loan.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: When one borrower is on the new loan casefile but more than one borrower is on the existing loan, the DU message will state that it appears that a borrower is being removed with transaction and refer the lender to the Selling Guide for additional requirements on removing a borrower with a high LTV refinance transaction.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: When there is more than one borrower on the new loan casefile but there is only one on the existing loan, the DU message will state that it appears that a borrower is being added with the high LTV refinance transaction, and if that is the case, the high LTV refinance loan is not eligible for delivery.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Higher-Priced Loan Requirements If the loan is a higher-priced mortgage loan or a higherpriced covered transaction under Regulation Z, the loan must comply with the Alternative Qualification Path requirements. DU is unable to determine if a loan casefile is a higher-priced mortgage loan or a higher-priced covered transaction under Regulation Z. The lender must make this determination. Lenders are not relieved of complying with Regulation Z by only adhering to the stricter representative credit score and DTI ratio. The loan must comply in all respects with Regulation Z requirements for such loans, including the underwriting and consumer protection requirements.
elements.label: text
